title: acer negundo var. vulgare
intro: Acer negundo var. vulgare, also known as the boxelder or Manitoba maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, which can help treat various health issues. The health benefits of Acer negundo var. vulgare include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and sap can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
